things.name: St. John the Baptist Cathedral
things.description: Slovakia's most important baroque cathedral from 1637 with extraordinary stucco decoration, the relic of St. John the Almoner and a revered Black Madonna

things.name: Old Town Walls
things.description: Trnava is one of Slovakia's best preserved medieval walled towns; substantial stretches of 13th-century fortifications with towers remain standing

things.name: Trnava University
things.description: Founded in 1635 by Cardinal Pazman, Trnava University was the first Hungarian university; the historic campus and church are central to the city's identity

things.name: Main Square (Trojicne namestie)
things.description: Trnava's elegant baroque central square with the Trinity Column, surrounding merchant houses and the lively daily life of this compact city

things.name: West Slovak Museum
things.description: Regional museum with rich collections on the archaeology, art and history of western Slovakia, housed in a baroque palace in the old town

things.name: Trnava City Tower
things.description: Medieval tower that has stood as the symbol of Trnava since the 14th century, offering views over the terracotta rooftops of this Little Rome of Slovakia

things.name: Church of the Holy Trinity
things.description: Spectacular late-baroque Jesuit church with a grand colonnade facade and sumptuous interior that forms the dramatic centerpiece of the main square

things.name: Trnava Gallery
things.description: Municipal art gallery presenting works by Slovak artists with a particular emphasis on modern and contemporary Slovak visual art

things.name: Trnava Cultural Summer Festival
things.description: Annual summer festival filling the old town squares and courtyards with open-air concerts, theatre and cultural events from June to September

things.name: Anton Malatinsky Stadium
things.description: Home of FC Spartak Trnava, Slovakia's most successful club with eight league titles and history as a European competitor in the 1960s and 1970s
